abort.single_instance_allowed: Only a single configuration of Mail and Packages is allowed.

error.communication: Unable to connect or login to the mail server. Please check the log for details.
error.invalid_path: Please store the images in another directory.

user.description:
Enter your IMAP mail server connection details. This is the email account the integration will monitor for shipping notifications.

Common IMAP hosts: imap.gmail.com (Gmail), outlook.office365.com (Outlook), imap.mail.yahoo.com (Yahoo).

For Gmail, you must use an App Password (not your regular password). See your email provider's documentation for IMAP access details.
user.title: Mail and Packages - Mail Server Connection

reauth_confirm.description: The IMAP mail server credentials are no longer valid. Please re-enter your credentials to continue receiving mail and package updates.
reauth_confirm.title: Mail and Packages - Re-authenticate

config_2.description:
Select which mailbox folder to monitor and which carrier sensors to create.

Most users should select "INBOX". Choose only the carriers you actually receive shipments from to keep things clean.

The "zpackages" sensors are totals that automatically sum across all enabled carriers.
config_2.title: Mail and Packages - Mailbox & Sensors

data.amazon_days: Days Back to Check Amazon Emails

config_3.description:
Configure how often to check for new mail and your Amazon settings.

A scan interval of 5-30 minutes is recommended. Lower values check more frequently but increase server load.

**Amazon forwarding**: If you forward Amazon shipping emails to a separate address, enter those addresses here separated by commas. Do NOT enter an @amazon.com address - only your forwarding address(es).
config_3.title: Mail and Packages - Scanning & Amazon

config_4.description:
Configure USPS Informed Delivery image handling.

The integration creates an animated GIF from USPS mail scan images. Adjust the frame duration to control playback speed.

**Generate MP4**: Creates an MP4 video alongside the GIF. Requires ffmpeg to be installed.

**Copy to www/**: Makes images accessible at /local/ URLs for use with notification apps. Only enable if needed - this makes images publicly accessible on your network.

**Custom image**: Use your own image when there is no mail to display.
config_4.title: Mail and Packages - Image Settings

data.scan_all_emails: Scan All Emails for Tracking Numbers
data.tracking_forward_enabled: Forward Tracking Numbers to a Service

config_5.description:
Enable optional advanced features. All are disabled by default and all processing is local unless otherwise noted.

**Scan all emails**: Searches all emails (not just known carriers) for tracking number patterns. Runs locally.

**Forward tracking**: Sends discovered tracking numbers to a tracking service like 17track, AfterShip, or AliExpress. Requires internet access.

**AI/LLM analysis**: Uses an AI model to identify tracking numbers in emails. Ollama runs locally; other providers send data externally.

**Amazon cookies**: Tracks Amazon orders directly using browser session cookies instead of email parsing.
config_5.title: Mail and Packages - Advanced Features

config_advanced.description:
Configure the advanced tracking features you enabled.

**Tracking Service**: For 17track, enter the config entry ID (Settings > Devices & Services > 17Track > three-dot menu > Copy entry ID). AfterShip and AliExpress do not require an entry ID.

**AI/LLM**: Ollama (http://localhost:11434) keeps everything local. Anthropic and OpenAI send email content to their cloud APIs.

**PRIVACY NOTICE**: Anthropic and OpenAI will receive your email content. Only use if you accept this. Ollama keeps everything on your network.

**Amazon Cookies**: Paste session cookies from your browser's developer tools (key=value; key2=value2 format).
config_advanced.title: Mail and Packages - Advanced Configuration

config_6.description:
Enter the path to your custom 'no mail' image. This image is shown when there are no USPS Informed Delivery mail scans available.

Example: images/custom_nomail.gif
config_6.title: Mail and Packages - Custom Image
